title: Actors not working when sidecar requires `dapr-api-token`
body:
<!-- If you need to report a security issue please visit https://docs.dapr.io/operations/support/support-security-issues -->

## In what area(s)?

<!-- Remove the '> ' to select -->

> /area runtime

> /area operator

> /area placement

> /area docs

> /area test-and-release

## What version of Dapr?

<!-- Delete all but your choice -->

> 1.1.x
> 1.0.x
> edge: output of `git describe --dirty`

1.14.x

Most likely present for many versions, if not always.

## Expected Behavior

When a sidecar requires `dapr-api-token`, actor invocation should still work in standalone mode.

Might happen on K8s too, did not test.

## Actual Behavior

Actor invocation doesn't work if`dapr-api-token` is required by the sidecar hosting the actor type.

Bug identified in Java SDK integration tests when forced all sidecars to require `dapr-api-token` but had to exclude apps that host actor types: https://github.com/dapr/java-sdk/pull/1142

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

1. In standalone more, run an app with sidecar where ``dapr-api-token`=anything` env is set and hosts an actor type.
2. Then, run another app that does not have the env set and DOES NOT host any actor type.
3. App in (2) must also invoke an actor method hosted by (1).
4. See error.
5. Then, unset `dapr-api-token` in (1) and repeat. See that it now works.

## Release Note

<!-- How should the fix for this issue be communicated in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE: **FIXED** Sidecar to host actor types even when `dapr-api-token` is required

comments.body: Fixing this might be tricky as the sidecars must know each other's tokens and have an authorization layer below, to make sure app's only invoke the ones that they have permission to. Another way is if all the sidecars communicate via a bi-directional streaming of a centralized "hub" control plane - also tricky.

Maybe there is a solution where placement service holds the tokens for each sidecar instance. It means that each sidecar instance will share its token with placement on registration. Still, vulnerable to a memory dump in placement.
